points.text: Neomorph Inc. closed $100 million Series B financing led by Deerfield Management to advance its molecular glue degrader pipeline and ongoing Phase 1/2 trial of NEO-811 for clear cell renal cell carcinoma.

Neomorph — NEO-811 — Locally advanced or metastatic non-resectable clear cell renal cell carcinoma — Phase 1/2 first-in-human open-label monotherapy trial ongoing; $100M Series B financing secured to support clinical execution and broader molecular glue degrader pipeline advancement.

Neomorph — Pioneering molecular glue degrader platform for undruggable proteins — NEO-811 + broader pipeline — $100M Series B closed April 2026; partnerships established with Novo Nordisk, Biogen, and AbbVie across oncology, cardiometabolic, and neurology indications.

NEO-811 — Mechanism: Molecular glue degrader targeting previously undruggable proteins — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Locally advanced or metastatic non-resectable clear cell renal cell carcinoma — Study design: First-in-human open-label monotherapy assessing safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and preliminary anti-tumor activity.

Neomorph — Secured $100 million Series B financing led by Deerfield Management with participation from Regeneron Ventures, Longwood Fund, Alexandria Venture Investments, and Binney Street Capital to fund NEO-811 clinical development and molecular glue platform expansion.
